hero.title: Identify
hero.subtitle: Global Identification Keys
hero.description: The foundation of the entire GS1 System. A universal language of unique identifiers that gives every product, location, and asset a distinct digital identity recognized across 116+ countries.

overview.title: One Product. One Number. Recognized Everywhere.
paragraphs: In today's interconnected economy, every product needs a universally recognized identity. GS1 Identification Keys provide that foundation — assigning a unique, globally unambiguous number to every trade item, logistics unit, location, and service relationship.

Within the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, GS1 identification is not just a best practice — it is a regulatory requirement for sectors including pharmaceuticals (SFDA), food safety, and retail. By adopting GS1 Keys, Saudi businesses unlock seamless access to domestic and international markets.

Each key is structured to prevent duplication across the entire global supply chain, ensuring that whether a product is scanned in Riyadh, Rotterdam, or Rio de Janeiro, the same accurate information is retrieved instantly.

components.name: Global Trade Item Number
components.description: The world's most widely used product identifier. Every retail product, from a bottle of water to a luxury watch, carries a GTIN encoded in its barcode. It is mandatory for listing on Amazon, Noon, and every major Saudi retailer.

components.name: Global Location Number
components.description: A 13-digit key that uniquely identifies physical locations (warehouses, stores, hospitals) and legal entities (companies, departments). GLN enables pinpoint accuracy in 'ship-to' and 'bill-to' processes across the Kingdom's logistics network.

components.name: Serial Shipping Container Code
components.description: Think of the SSCC as a digital license plate for every pallet, crate, or shipping container. It enables complete traceability of logistics units from the factory floor in Jubail to the retail shelf in Jeddah — and beyond to global export destinations.

components.name: Global Service Relation Number
components.description: Identifies the relationship between a service provider and a recipient. In KSA's healthcare sector, the GSRN is used to link patients to their medical records, prescriptions, and treatment histories across SFDA-regulated facilities.

components.name: Global Document Type Identifier
components.description: Enables unique identification of document types and individual documents. Widely used in customs automation through FASAH, corporate compliance, and administrative digitization initiatives aligned with Saudi Vision 2030.

steps.title: Join GS1 Saudi Arabia
steps.description: Register with GS1 KSA to receive your unique Company Prefix — the foundation of all your GS1 identification keys.

steps.title: Assign Your Keys
steps.description: Use your prefix to create GTINs for products, GLNs for locations, and SSCCs for shipments. Each key is globally unique.

steps.title: Encode & Apply
steps.description: Embed your identification keys into barcodes, QR codes, or RFID tags and apply them to products and packaging.

steps.title: Trade Globally
steps.description: Your products are now recognized by retailers, marketplaces, and regulatory bodies in 116+ countries worldwide.

benefits.title: Why Leading Saudi Businesses Choose GS1 Identification

items.title: Global Market Access
items.description: GTINs are required by Amazon, Noon, Carrefour, and every major global retailer. Without them, your products simply cannot be listed.

items.title: Regulatory Compliance
items.description: Meet SFDA, SASO, and Zakat Authority requirements. GS1 keys are the foundation of pharmaceutical serialization and food traceability mandates.

items.title: Operational Efficiency
items.description: Eliminate manual data entry errors. Automated scanning of GS1 keys reduces processing time by up to 80% in warehouses and at checkout.

items.title: Data Integrity
items.description: One source of truth. GS1 keys ensure that every partner in your supply chain references the exact same product, location, or shipment.

items.title: Consumer Confidence
items.description: Customers trust products they can verify. GS1 identification enables product authentication and anti-counterfeiting measures.

items.title: Vision 2030 Alignment
items.description: Support the Kingdom's digital transformation goals through standardized, interoperable identification across every sector of the economy.

items.name: Retail & FMCG
items.detail: Mandatory GTIN for every product on every shelf and every e-commerce listing.

items.name: Healthcare & Pharma
items.detail: SFDA-mandated serialization using GTIN + Serial Number for drug traceability.

items.name: Logistics & Transport
items.detail: SSCC enables pallet-level tracking across the Kingdom's entire logistics network.

items.name: Food & Agriculture
items.detail: Farm-to-fork traceability meeting SFDA food safety regulations.

faq.question: What is the difference between a GTIN and a barcode?
faq.answer: A GTIN is the number (the unique identifier). A barcode is the visual carrier (the lines or dots) that encodes that number so machines can read it. Think of it like the difference between your phone number and the contact card that stores it.

faq.question: Is a GTIN mandatory for selling in Saudi Arabia?
faq.answer: Yes. All retail products sold in KSA must carry a valid GTIN. It is required by major retailers (Panda, Tamimi, Carrefour) and e-commerce platforms (Amazon.sa, Noon). SFDA also requires GTINs for pharmaceutical products.

faq.question: How long does it take to get a GS1 Company Prefix?
faq.answer: After completing registration and payment with GS1 Saudi Arabia, your Company Prefix is typically issued within 2-3 business days.

faq.question: Can I use the same GTIN globally?
faq.answer: Absolutely. That is the entire purpose of GS1. A GTIN assigned in Saudi Arabia is recognized and valid in all 116+ GS1 member countries. No re-registration is needed.

cta.title: Ready to Identify Your Products Globally?
cta.description: Join thousands of Saudi businesses already using GS1 identification keys to access domestic and international markets.

hero.title: Capture
hero.subtitle: Data Carriers — Barcodes & RFID
hero.description: The bridge between the physical and digital worlds. GS1 data carriers encode identification keys into barcodes, QR codes, and RFID tags — enabling machines to read product data in milliseconds.

overview.title: Every Scan Tells a Story.
paragraphs: Data capture is the mechanism that transforms static product identification into actionable, real-time information. When a barcode is scanned at a Saudi supermarket checkout, a warehouse dock, or a hospital pharmacy — data flows instantly into digital systems, eliminating manual entry and human error.

GS1 data carriers come in multiple formats designed for specific environments: linear barcodes for high-speed retail scanning, 2D DataMatrix codes for pharmaceutical serialization, and RFID tags for bulk inventory management without line-of-sight scanning.

The future is GS1 Digital Link — a QR code that connects consumers directly to rich product information, authenticity verification, nutritional data, and brand experiences, all through a simple smartphone scan.

components.name: Linear Barcodes
components.description: The classic barcode you see on every retail product worldwide. EAN-13 (used in KSA) and UPC-A (used in North America) encode the GTIN for lightning-fast point-of-sale scanning. Over 10 billion scans happen globally every single day.

components.name: Application Identifier Barcodes
components.description: An advanced linear barcode that encodes not just the GTIN, but also batch numbers, expiry dates, serial numbers, and weight. It is the standard for logistics labels and regulated supply chains.

components.name: GS1 DataMatrix
components.description: A 2D barcode that packs massive amounts of data into a tiny space. Mandatory for SFDA pharmaceutical serialization in Saudi Arabia. It encodes GTIN, serial number, batch, and expiry — enabling full drug traceability from manufacturer to patient.

components.name: GS1 Digital Link QR Code
components.description: The next generation of barcodes. A single QR code that works at the checkout scanner AND connects consumers to dynamic digital content — product pages, nutritional info, authenticity checks, and brand experiences. One code, infinite possibilities.

components.name: Electronic Product Code / RFID
components.description: Radio Frequency Identification enables scanning without line-of-sight. Read hundreds of tagged items simultaneously. Transforming inventory management in Saudi retail, logistics, and healthcare with real-time visibility.
useCases: Bulk inventory scanning (no line of sight)

process.title: From Physical Product to Digital Data

steps.title: Choose Your Carrier
steps.description: Select the right data carrier for your use case — linear barcode for retail, DataMatrix for pharma, RFID for logistics, or Digital Link for consumer engagement.

steps.title: Encode Your Data
steps.description: Embed your GS1 identification keys (GTIN, GLN, SSCC) along with supplementary data (batch, expiry, serial) into the chosen carrier format.

steps.title: Print or Apply
steps.description: Generate high-quality barcodes for product packaging, shipping labels, or embed RFID tags directly into products or pallets.

steps.title: Scan & Activate
steps.description: Every scan triggers instant data capture — updating POS systems, warehouse management, regulatory databases, and consumer-facing applications in real time.

benefits.title: Why GS1 Data Carriers Power the World's Supply Chains

items.title: Lightning-Fast Processing
items.description: A single barcode scan replaces 20+ keystrokes of manual data entry. RFID can read 1,000+ items per second without line of sight.

items.title: Zero Error Rate
items.description: Manual data entry has a 1-in-300 error rate. Barcode scanning achieves 1 error in 36 trillion characters scanned.

items.title: SFDA Compliance
items.description: GS1 DataMatrix is the mandated carrier for pharmaceutical serialization in Saudi Arabia. Non-compliance means market exclusion.

items.title: Consumer Engagement
items.description: GS1 Digital Link QR codes turn every product into a digital touchpoint — connecting consumers to rich brand experiences.

items.title: Real-Time Visibility
items.description: Every scan creates a data event. Track products from factory to shelf in real time across the entire Kingdom.

items.title: Future-Proof Technology
items.description: GS1 standards evolve with technology. From 1D barcodes to RFID to Digital Link, your investment is always current.

items.name: Retail & E-commerce
items.detail: 10 billion daily scans at checkout. EAN/UPC is the universal language of retail.

items.name: Healthcare & Pharma
items.detail: SFDA-mandated DataMatrix for drug serialization, patient safety, and recall management.

items.name: Logistics & Warehousing
items.detail: GS1-128 and RFID for automated receiving, picking, and shipping across KSA.

items.name: Consumer Goods
items.detail: Digital Link QR codes connecting every product to rich digital consumer experiences.

faq.question: What is the difference between a 1D barcode and a 2D barcode?
faq.answer: A 1D (linear) barcode uses parallel lines to encode data — typically just a GTIN number. A 2D barcode (DataMatrix, QR Code) uses patterns of squares to encode much more data — including batch numbers, expiry dates, serial numbers, and even URLs.

faq.question: Is GS1 DataMatrix mandatory for pharmaceuticals in KSA?
faq.answer: Yes. The Saudi Food & Drug Authority (SFDA) mandates GS1 DataMatrix encoding for pharmaceutical serialization. Every drug package must carry a unique serial number encoded in a GS1 DataMatrix barcode.

faq.question: What is GS1 Digital Link and why should I care?
faq.answer: GS1 Digital Link is a web-enabled QR code standard. Instead of just encoding a number, it encodes a URL that resolves to dynamic product information. One scan can serve the checkout scanner, the consumer, and the regulator simultaneously.

faq.question: Can RFID replace barcodes entirely?
faq.answer: Not yet. RFID and barcodes are complementary technologies. RFID excels at bulk scanning and real-time tracking, while barcodes remain more cost-effective for individual product identification. Most leading supply chains use both.

cta.title: Modernize Your Data Capture Systems
cta.description: From linear barcodes to RFID to Digital Link — our experts will help you choose and implement the right GS1 data carriers.

hero.title: Share
hero.subtitle: Data Interchange — EDI, GDSN & EPCIS
hero.description: The power of GS1 is fully realized when data flows seamlessly between partners. Share standards enable automated, accurate, and secure exchange of product, transaction, and event data across the entire supply chain.

overview.title: Connected Data. Connected Supply Chains.
paragraphs: Identification and capture are only valuable when the data reaches the right partner at the right time. GS1 Share standards create the digital highways that connect manufacturers, distributors, retailers, regulators, and consumers in a seamless data ecosystem.

In Saudi Arabia, where supply chains span from local farms to global export markets, standardized data sharing eliminates the costly errors, delays, and inefficiencies caused by manual processes and proprietary data formats. Every trading partner speaks the same digital language.

From automated purchase orders via EDI, to synchronized product master data via GDSN, to real-time event visibility via EPCIS — GS1 Share standards transform fragmented supply chains into intelligent, responsive networks ready for the demands of Vision 2030.

components.name: Global Data Synchronisation Network
components.description: The world's largest product data network. GDSN ensures that the product information a manufacturer publishes (ingredients, dimensions, images, allergens) is the exact same information retailers display on shelves and online. One change updates everywhere, automatically.

components.name: Electronic Data Interchange
components.description: The backbone of paperless B2B commerce. EDI automates the exchange of business documents — Purchase Orders, Invoices, Advance Shipping Notices, and Payment Advices — between trading partners in a standardized, machine-readable format.

components.name: Electronic Product Code Information Services
components.description: The visibility standard. EPCIS captures and shares supply chain events — answering What happened, Where it happened, When it happened, and Why. It is the foundation of traceability, recall management, and regulatory compliance.

components.name: Global Data Model
components.description: A unified framework that structures product data consistently across all channels — physical retail, e-commerce, mobile apps, and digital catalogs. The GDM ensures that product data is complete, accurate, and formatted correctly for every touchpoint.

process.title: From Isolated Data to Connected Intelligence

steps.title: Standardize Your Data
steps.description: Structure your product, location, and transaction data according to GS1 global standards using the Global Data Model.

steps.title: Publish & Synchronize
steps.description: Upload your product master data to the GDSN. Trading partners subscribe and receive automatic updates whenever data changes.

steps.title: Automate Transactions
steps.description: Replace manual ordering, invoicing, and shipping documentation with EDI. Orders flow in minutes instead of days.

steps.title: Gain Full Visibility
steps.description: Implement EPCIS to capture and share supply chain events. Every partner sees the same real-time view of product movements.

benefits.title: Why Shared Data Transforms Supply Chains

items.title: Trusted Product Data
items.description: GDSN ensures retailers display the manufacturer's verified data — correct ingredients, allergens, images, and descriptions. No discrepancies.

items.title: Automated Commerce
items.description: EDI reduces order-to-delivery cycle time by up to 65%. Purchase orders that took days now complete in minutes.

items.title: End-to-End Traceability
items.description: EPCIS enables farm-to-fork and factory-to-patient traceability. In a recall, affected products are identified in hours, not weeks.

items.title: Cost Reduction
items.description: Automated data sharing eliminates manual data entry, reducing processing costs by up to 80% and cutting invoice disputes by 50%.

items.title: Regulatory Readiness
items.description: SFDA, SASO, and international regulators increasingly require standardized data sharing. GS1 share standards ensure full compliance.

items.title: Consumer Transparency
items.description: Consumers can trace products back to their source. Shared data builds brand trust and supports sustainability reporting.

items.name: Retail & FMCG
items.detail: GDSN synchronization ensures accurate shelf labels, online listings, and promotional data.

items.name: Healthcare & Pharma
items.detail: EPCIS-based traceability for drug authenticity and patient safety compliance.

items.name: Logistics & 3PL
items.detail: EDI-automated ASN, invoicing, and proof-of-delivery across the Kingdom's logistics network.

items.name: Government & Customs
items.detail: Standardized data interchange with FASAH and other government digital services.

faq.question: What is GDSN and why do I need it?
faq.answer: GDSN (Global Data Synchronisation Network) is a network of certified data pools that enables manufacturers and retailers to exchange standardized product data. You need it because retailers increasingly require GDSN for product onboarding — and it ensures your product data is accurate everywhere it appears.

faq.question: How is EDI different from email or PDF invoices?
faq.answer: EDI is machine-to-machine communication. Unlike email or PDF, EDI documents are structured data that flows directly into ERP and accounting systems without human intervention. This eliminates manual data entry, reduces errors by 80%+, and speeds up processing from days to minutes.

faq.question: What supply chain events does EPCIS track?
faq.answer: EPCIS tracks four dimensions of every supply chain event: WHAT (which products), WHERE (which locations), WHEN (timestamps), and WHY (business context like shipping, receiving, or transforming). This creates a complete, auditable history of every product's journey.

faq.question: Can small businesses benefit from GS1 sharing standards?
faq.answer: Absolutely. GS1 KSA offers tiered solutions. Even small businesses benefit from GDSN (faster retailer onboarding), EDI (automated ordering), and EPCIS (traceability). Many cloud-based solutions make implementation accessible and affordable.

cta.title: Connect Your Supply Chain to the World
cta.description: Implement GS1 sharing standards to automate transactions, synchronize data, and gain full supply chain visibility.
